pillars.name: Pillar 1: Personal Leadership (Leading Yourself First)
pillars.subtitle: The Person — Inner mastery pillar

dims.name: Self-Awareness
questions: I can clearly identify my top 3 strengths and top 3 weaknesses as a leader.
I regularly seek honest feedback from others about my leadership style.
I understand how my personality type affects the way I lead.
I am aware of my emotional triggers and how they impact my decisions.

dims.name: Emotional Intelligence
questions: I can remain calm and composed when under pressure or facing criticism.
I am able to read the emotions of others and respond appropriately.
I manage frustration and anger constructively rather than reactively.
I show genuine empathy when team members face personal challenges.

dims.name: Leading Yourself (Discipline, Habits, Character)
questions: I consistently follow through on commitments I make to myself and others.
I have daily disciplines (reading, prayer, exercise, reflection) that I maintain.
My private life reflects the same standards I hold publicly.
I take ownership of my mistakes rather than blaming others or circumstances.

dims.name: Leadership Styles — Situational Leadership
questions: I adapt my leadership approach depending on the situation and the person.
I know when to be directive and when to step back and empower others.
I can identify whether a team member needs coaching, support, or delegation.
I am comfortable leading in both structured and ambiguous environments.

dims.name: Decision-Making
questions: I make decisions confidently even when I don't have all the information.
I consider the long-term consequences of my decisions, not just short-term wins.
I involve the right people in decisions without being paralysed by consensus.
I can explain the reasoning behind my decisions clearly to my team.
I learn from past decisions and adjust my approach accordingly.

pillars.name: Pillar 2: Directional Leadership (Where Are We Going?)
pillars.subtitle: The Principles — Results and clarity pillar

dims.name: Vision
questions: I have a clear, compelling vision for where I am leading my team or organisation.
I can articulate my vision in a way that excites and inspires others.
My daily actions are aligned with my long-term vision.
I regularly revisit and refine my vision based on new insights.

dims.name: Strategic Planning & Execution
questions: I have a documented plan with clear milestones for achieving my goals.
I can break down big goals into actionable steps with timelines.
I regularly review progress against my plan and make adjustments.
I distinguish between urgent tasks and strategically important ones.

dims.name: Goal Setting (SMART)
questions: I set specific, measurable goals rather than vague intentions.
My goals have clear deadlines and I track my progress regularly.
I set goals that stretch me but are still realistic and achievable.
I write my goals down and review them at least weekly.

dims.name: Accountability & Ownership
questions: I have an accountability partner or mentor who challenges me regularly.
I hold myself to the same standards I expect from others.
When a project fails, I look inward first before pointing outward.
I create systems of accountability within my team, not just for myself.

pillars.name: Pillar 3: Relational Leadership (How We Lead People)
pillars.subtitle: The Process — Culture pillar

dims.name: Communication
questions: I communicate clearly and concisely, avoiding ambiguity.
I listen actively — I seek to understand before being understood.
I tailor my communication style depending on my audience.
I give direct, honest communication even when the message is uncomfortable.
I create space for others to voice their opinions and concerns.

dims.name: Team Building
questions: I intentionally invest in building trust within my team.
I understand the strengths of each team member and position them accordingly.
I celebrate team wins and give credit where it's due.
I actively work to create a culture where people feel safe to contribute.

dims.name: Conflict Resolution
questions: I address conflict directly rather than avoiding it or hoping it resolves itself.
I can mediate disagreements between team members fairly.
I separate the person from the problem when navigating conflict.
I seek win-win outcomes rather than just winning the argument.

dims.name: Servant Leadership
questions: I regularly ask my team 'How can I help you succeed?'
I prioritise the growth and wellbeing of my team over my own advancement.
I lead by example — I don't ask others to do what I wouldn't do myself.
I use my position to serve others, not to gain power or status.

pillars.name: Pillar 4: Performance Leadership (Driving Standards)
pillars.subtitle: Execution discipline pillar

dims.name: Delegation
questions: I delegate tasks based on people's strengths, not just availability.
I give clear instructions and expected outcomes when I delegate.
I trust my team to execute without micromanaging the process.
I follow up on delegated tasks without hovering over people.

dims.name: Feedback & Performance Management
questions: I give constructive feedback regularly, not just during annual reviews.
I balance positive recognition with honest developmental feedback.
I create a culture where feedback flows in both directions.
I use specific examples when giving feedback, not generalisations.

dims.name: Managing Underperformance
questions: I address underperformance early rather than letting it build up.
I distinguish between a skill gap and a motivation gap when someone underperforms.
I document performance issues and create improvement plans.
I am willing to make tough personnel decisions when necessary.

dims.name: Difficult Conversations
questions: I can have hard conversations without becoming emotional or aggressive.
I prepare for difficult conversations rather than winging them.
I am direct but respectful when delivering unwelcome news.
I follow up after difficult conversations to check on the person and the outcome.

pillars.name: Pillar 5: Multiplication & Impact (Beyond You)
pillars.subtitle: Expansion pillar

dims.name: Coaching
questions: I regularly coach others by asking powerful questions rather than just giving answers.
I help people discover their own solutions instead of creating dependency on me.
I invest time in one-on-one development conversations with emerging leaders.
I can identify someone's potential even before they see it themselves.

dims.name: Mentoring
questions: I have mentees who I am intentionally pouring into.
I share my failures and lessons learned, not just my successes.
I connect my mentees with other resources and people who can help them grow.
I adjust my mentoring approach based on each person's unique needs and stage.

dims.name: Developing Leaders
questions: I actively identify and develop future leaders within my team or organisation.
I give emerging leaders real responsibility and authority, not just tasks.
I have a leadership pipeline — people at different stages of development.
I measure my success by the leaders I produce, not just the results I deliver.

dims.name: Legacy
questions: I regularly think about the long-term impact of my leadership beyond my tenure.
I am building something that will outlast me, not just something that depends on me.
I document my leadership principles and frameworks for others to learn from.
I invest in institutions and systems, not just individual relationships.

pillars.name: Pillar 1: Personal Foundation (The Minister as a Person)
pillars.subtitle: Inner life determines outer fruit

dims.name: Calling Clarity
questions: I can clearly articulate why God has called me to ministry.
I have peace and confidence about my specific ministry assignment.
I know the difference between my calling and my current role or title.
I can discern when a season of ministry is ending and a new one is beginning.

dims.name: Spiritual Disciplines
questions: I maintain a consistent personal prayer life independent of public ministry.
I regularly fast and practice spiritual disciplines beyond Sunday services.
I study Scripture for my own growth, not just for sermon preparation.
I practice solitude and silence as part of my spiritual rhythm.

dims.name: Character & Integrity
questions: My private life reflects the same values I preach publicly.
I am transparent with at least one trusted person about my struggles.
I handle money, power, and relationships with integrity and accountability.
I would be comfortable if my congregation saw every area of my life.

dims.name: Emotional & Mental Health
questions: I recognise the signs of burnout in myself and take proactive steps.
I have healthy boundaries between ministry demands and personal rest.
I process grief, disappointment, and failure in healthy ways.
I have a support system outside of my ministry context.
I would seek professional help if my emotional health required it.

pillars.name: Pillar 2: Theological & Doctrinal Depth (What You Believe)
pillars.subtitle: Sound doctrine shapes sound ministry

dims.name: Biblical Literacy
questions: I have a systematic approach to studying the entire Bible, not just favourite passages.
I can explain the major themes and narrative arc of Scripture confidently.
I use sound hermeneutical principles when interpreting Scripture.
I regularly study original languages or use tools that help me understand them.

dims.name: Doctrinal Conviction
questions: I can clearly articulate my core theological beliefs and why I hold them.
I distinguish between essential doctrines and secondary matters of opinion.
I have studied church history enough to understand how my beliefs developed.
I can respectfully engage with theological positions different from my own.

dims.name: Contextual Theology
questions: I apply biblical truth to the specific cultural context of my congregation.
I address real-life issues (poverty, corruption, family dynamics) through Scripture.
I am developing a theology that speaks to the African/local experience.
I avoid simply importing foreign theological frameworks without contextualisation.

dims.name: Apologetics & Critical Thinking
questions: I can defend the Christian faith against common objections and questions.
I engage with contemporary culture and intellectual challenges thoughtfully.
I teach my congregation to think critically rather than just accept blindly.
I am comfortable saying 'I don't know' and committing to research an answer.

pillars.name: Pillar 3: Ministry Skills (How You Serve)
pillars.subtitle: The craft of ministry execution

dims.name: Preaching & Teaching
questions: I prepare my sermons thoroughly with clear structure and application.
I vary my preaching style and methods to engage different audiences.
I receive and act on feedback about my preaching effectiveness.
My sermons consistently move people toward transformation, not just information.
I invest in improving my communication skills through study and practice.

dims.name: Discipleship & Mentoring
questions: I have a clear discipleship pathway for new believers in my ministry.
I personally disciple a small group of people, not just preach to crowds.
I equip others to disciple others — I'm not the only discipler.
I measure success by spiritual maturity, not just attendance numbers.

dims.name: Pastoral Care & Counselling
questions: I make time for one-on-one pastoral conversations with my members.
I know when a situation requires professional counselling beyond my capacity.
I maintain confidentiality and trust in pastoral relationships.
I care for people in crisis (grief, illness, family breakdown) with presence and wisdom.

dims.name: Worship & Creative Arts
questions: I value and invest in the worship and creative arts ministry.
I work collaboratively with worship leaders to create meaningful services.
I understand that worship extends beyond music to a lifestyle.
I encourage creativity and excellence in how we present the gospel.

dims.name: Evangelism & Outreach
questions: I actively lead outreach efforts beyond the walls of the church.
I equip my congregation to share their faith naturally in everyday life.
I engage with the community around my church, not just my members.
I measure evangelistic fruitfulness, not just church attendance.

pillars.name: Pillar 4: Organisational Leadership (Running the House)
pillars.subtitle: Stewardship of people and resources

dims.name: Vision & Strategic Planning
questions: I have a clear 3-5 year vision for where this ministry is heading.
I have communicated this vision so clearly that my leaders can repeat it.
I have a strategic plan with measurable milestones, not just good intentions.
I regularly evaluate whether our activities align with our stated vision.

dims.name: Team Building & Volunteer Management
questions: I recruit volunteers based on their gifts, not just their availability.
I invest in training and developing my ministry team regularly.
I appreciate and celebrate my volunteers, not just use them.
I have a system for onboarding, equipping, and releasing new volunteers.

dims.name: Financial Stewardship
questions: My ministry has a transparent budget that the leadership team reviews regularly.
I teach biblical principles of giving and stewardship to my congregation.
I personally model generosity and financial integrity.
I ensure proper financial controls — multiple signatories, audits, reporting.

dims.name: Governance & Church Administration
questions: My church/ministry has a clear governance structure with defined roles.
I maintain proper records (membership, finances, meeting minutes).
I delegate administrative tasks rather than trying to do everything myself.
I use technology and systems to improve ministry efficiency.

pillars.name: Pillar 5: Sustainability & Multiplication (Beyond You)
pillars.subtitle: Ministry that outlives you

dims.name: Succession Planning
questions: I am actively preparing someone to take over my role if needed.
I share leadership responsibilities so the ministry doesn't depend solely on me.
I have a documented succession plan, even if informal.
I am emotionally willing to step aside when the time is right.

dims.name: Leader Development
questions: I invest in developing other ministers and leaders around me.
I give emerging leaders real platforms and opportunities, not just theory.
I celebrate when my trainees exceed my own abilities.
I have a leadership pipeline with people at various stages of development.

dims.name: Family & Ministry Balance
questions: My spouse and family feel valued, not sacrificed for ministry.
I take regular days off and vacations without guilt.
I set boundaries on ministry demands to protect my family time.
My children (if applicable) have a healthy view of ministry and the church.

dims.name: Legacy & Kingdom Impact
questions: I think about the long-term impact of my ministry beyond my own lifetime.
I invest in institutions (schools, programmes, networks) not just events.
I measure success by transformation in people's lives, not just activities.
I am building a ministry that will thrive even after I'm gone.

pillars.name: Pillar 1: Personal Foundation (Me Before We)
pillars.subtitle: A healthy marriage starts with two healthy individuals

dims.name: Self-Awareness
questions: I understand my own emotional triggers and how they affect my spouse.
I can identify patterns from my family of origin that I bring into my marriage.
I am honest with myself about my contribution to our marital challenges.
I regularly reflect on my own growth areas rather than focusing on my spouse's flaws.

dims.name: Emotional Intelligence
questions: I can manage my emotions during disagreements without shutting down or exploding.
I recognise when my spouse is struggling emotionally even if they don't say it.
I take responsibility for my emotional reactions rather than blaming my spouse.
I can pause and regulate myself before responding in heated moments.

dims.name: Personal Responsibility
questions: I own my mistakes in the relationship without making excuses.
I apologise sincerely when I am wrong, without adding 'but you also...'
I work on myself consistently, not just when there's a crisis.
I seek personal growth (books, counselling, mentoring) for the sake of my marriage.

dims.name: Spiritual & Value Alignment
questions: My spouse and I share the same core values and life priorities.
We pray together or engage in shared spiritual practices regularly.
We agree on how faith influences our decisions as a couple.
We have discussed and aligned on our views about money, parenting, and purpose.

pillars.name: Pillar 2: Communication & Connection (How We Talk)
pillars.subtitle: Most marriage problems are communication problems

dims.name: Listening & Empathy
questions: I listen to my spouse without interrupting or planning my response.
I try to understand my spouse's perspective even when I disagree.
My spouse would say I make them feel truly heard.
I ask clarifying questions rather than assuming I know what my spouse means.

dims.name: Conflict Resolution
questions: We resolve conflicts without resorting to insults, stonewalling, or silent treatment.
We can disagree without it turning into a personal attack.
We have an agreed approach for handling arguments (time-outs, ground rules).
After a conflict, we reconcile and don't let resentment build up.
We fight about the actual issue, not about past unresolved grievances.

dims.name: Emotional Intimacy
questions: I feel safe being vulnerable and honest with my spouse.
We share our fears, dreams, and inner thoughts with each other.
We have deep conversations beyond logistics and daily schedules.
I trust my spouse with my weaknesses and insecurities.

dims.name: Appreciation & Affirmation
questions: I regularly express gratitude and appreciation to my spouse.
I know my spouse's love language and I speak it intentionally.
I compliment my spouse genuinely, not just when I want something.
I celebrate my spouse's achievements and encourage their growth.

pillars.name: Pillar 3: Partnership & Roles (How We Function)
pillars.subtitle: Running a life together requires alignment

dims.name: Decision-Making Together
questions: We make major decisions together as equal partners.
Neither of us makes unilateral decisions that affect the other without consultation.
We have a fair process for resolving disagreements on decisions.
We respect each other's areas of strength when dividing decision-making.

dims.name: Financial Partnership
questions: We have open, honest conversations about money without shame or blame.
We have a shared budget or financial plan that we both agree on.
We make financial decisions together rather than hiding spending.
We are aligned on our approach to saving, giving, and debt.

dims.name: Household & Parenting Alignment
questions: We share household responsibilities in a way that feels fair to both of us.
We agree on our parenting approach and present a united front to our children.
We discuss and adjust roles as our family situation changes.
Neither of us carries a disproportionate burden without acknowledgment.

dims.name: Extended Family Boundaries
questions: We have clear, agreed boundaries with our extended families.
My spouse feels prioritised over my parents and siblings.
We handle in-law conflicts as a team rather than siding with our own families.
We have discussed how much influence extended family has on our decisions.

pillars.name: Pillar 4: Intimacy & Sexuality (The Private Bond)
pillars.subtitle: The dimension most couples avoid but desperately need

dims.name: Physical Intimacy
questions: I am satisfied with the frequency and quality of our physical intimacy.
We are both comfortable initiating physical affection.
Physical intimacy in our marriage goes beyond just sexual intercourse.
We make time for physical connection even during busy or stressful seasons.

dims.name: Sexual Communication
questions: We can talk openly about our sexual needs without embarrassment.
I feel comfortable expressing what I enjoy and what I don't.
We address sexual concerns or dissatisfaction constructively.
Neither of us uses sex as a weapon, bargaining tool, or punishment.

dims.name: Romance & Intentionality
questions: We go on regular dates or create intentional couple time.
We still flirt, surprise each other, and keep romance alive.
I put effort into being attractive and attentive to my spouse.
We have fun together — our relationship isn't all responsibilities and problems.

dims.name: Trust & Fidelity
questions: I trust my spouse completely with their friendships and interactions.
We have agreed boundaries around social media, phones, and opposite-sex friendships.
There are no secrets between us that would damage our relationship if revealed.
We protect our marriage by being transparent about where we go and who we see.

pillars.name: Pillar 5: Growth & Legacy (Where We're Going)
pillars.subtitle: A marriage with no vision drifts

dims.name: Shared Vision & Goals
questions: We have a shared vision for where our marriage and family is heading.
We set goals together — financial, relational, spiritual, family goals.
We regularly check in on our shared vision and adjust as needed.
We dream together about the future rather than just surviving the present.

dims.name: Crisis Resilience
questions: We have navigated a major crisis together and come out stronger.
We turn to each other (not away from each other) when times are hard.
We have external support (mentors, counsellors, community) for tough seasons.
We view challenges as opportunities to grow closer, not reasons to give up.

dims.name: Spiritual Partnership
questions: We pray for each other regularly.
We grow spiritually together, not just individually.
We serve others together as a couple — our marriage has an outward purpose.
We discuss spiritual insights, lessons, and convictions with each other.

dims.name: Legacy & Impact
questions: We are intentional about the example we set for our children.
We mentor or support other couples in their marriages.
We are building a family culture and values that will outlast us.
Our marriage is a source of strength and inspiration to those around us.

pillars.name: Pillar 1: Entrepreneurial Identity (The Founder)
pillars.subtitle: Who you are determines how you build

dims.name: Entrepreneurial Mindset
questions: I see obstacles as opportunities rather than reasons to quit.
I am comfortable with calculated risk and uncertainty.
I have a growth mindset — I believe my abilities can be developed.
I am driven by creating value, not just making money.

dims.name: Self-Leadership & Discipline
questions: I have structured daily routines that drive productivity.
I manage my time effectively and avoid chronic procrastination.
I can work independently without needing external motivation or supervision.
I maintain focus on priorities even when exciting distractions appear.
I take care of my physical and mental health as a business investment.

dims.name: Purpose & Calling
questions: I can clearly articulate why I started this business beyond making money.
My business aligns with my personal values and sense of purpose.
I see my business as a vehicle for Kingdom impact and community transformation.
I would continue this work even if the financial rewards were modest.

dims.name: Resilience & Mental Health
questions: I recover quickly from business setbacks and failures.
I have a support system (mentors, peers, family) that sustains me.
I manage the loneliness and pressure of entrepreneurship in healthy ways.
I know when to take a break and I actually do it.

pillars.name: Pillar 2: Business Foundations (The Model)
pillars.subtitle: The brutal truth about whether your business works

dims.name: Offer Clarity
questions: I can explain what my business does in one clear sentence.
My ideal customer can immediately understand the value I provide.
I have a defined, focused product or service — not a confusing menu of options.
I know exactly what problem I solve and for whom.

dims.name: Revenue Model & Pricing
questions: I have a clear, documented revenue model that shows how I make money.
My pricing reflects the true value I deliver, not just what I think people can pay.
I have multiple revenue streams or a plan to develop them.
I review and adjust my pricing at least annually based on data.
I understand my unit economics — cost to deliver vs. revenue per customer.

dims.name: Market Positioning
questions: I know exactly who my target customer is and can describe them in detail.
I can clearly articulate why a customer should choose me over my competitors.
I have researched my market and know the competitive landscape.
I have a unique selling proposition that differentiates my business.

dims.name: Legal & Compliance
questions: My business is formally registered and compliant with local regulations.
I have proper contracts, terms of service, and legal documentation.
I understand the tax obligations for my business and meet them.
I protect my intellectual property (trademarks, copyrights, patents where applicable).

pillars.name: Pillar 3: Execution & Operations (The Machine)
pillars.subtitle: Ideas are cheap — execution is everything

dims.name: Systems & Processes
questions: I have documented standard operating procedures for key business activities.
My business can function for at least a week without my direct involvement.
I use automation and tools to reduce repetitive manual work.
I regularly review and improve my operational processes.

dims.name: Product/Service Delivery
questions: My customers consistently receive a high-quality experience.
I have a reliable delivery process that doesn't depend entirely on me.
I collect and act on customer feedback to improve my offerings.
I can handle an increase in demand without quality dropping significantly.

dims.name: Financial Management
questions: I separate my personal and business finances completely.
I know my monthly cash flow — money in vs. money out — at all times.
I have a budget and I review my financial statements regularly.
I understand my profit margins and work to improve them.
I plan for taxes, insurance, and unexpected expenses.

dims.name: Technology & Tools
questions: I use appropriate technology to run my business efficiently.
I have a professional online presence (website, social media, email).
I use a CRM or system to manage customer relationships.
I invest in tools that save time and improve quality.

pillars.name: Pillar 4: Growth & Sales (The Engine)
pillars.subtitle: No sales = no business

dims.name: Sales Strategy & Pipeline
questions: I have a defined sales process from lead to close.
I consistently generate new leads and don't rely on one source of business.
I follow up on prospects systematically rather than sporadically.
I can sell my product or service with confidence and without feeling 'salesy'.
I track my sales metrics — conversion rates, average deal size, sales cycle.

dims.name: Marketing & Brand
questions: I have a clear, consistent brand identity (visual, voice, values).
I create valuable content that attracts my ideal customers.
I invest in marketing consistently, not just when sales are down.
I know which marketing channels give me the best return on investment.

dims.name: Customer Retention
questions: I have a strategy for keeping existing customers and increasing their lifetime value.
I actively seek referrals from satisfied customers.
I resolve customer complaints quickly and professionally.
I nurture customer relationships beyond the initial transaction.

dims.name: Partnerships & Networking
questions: I actively build strategic partnerships that open new opportunities.
I attend events, join communities, and build relationships intentionally.
I have collaborations that multiply my reach without multiplying my costs.
I give value to my network, not just extract from it.

dims.name: Scaling & Expansion
questions: I have a clear plan for scaling my business to the next level.
I have identified what needs to change for my business to grow 10x.
I am willing to let go of tasks and roles to focus on growth leadership.
I think regionally or globally, not just locally.

pillars.name: Pillar 5: Stewardship & Impact (The Legacy)
pillars.subtitle: Kingdom entrepreneurship is about more than profit

dims.name: Financial Stewardship
questions: I practise generosity and tithing as a business owner.
I pursue ethical profit — I won't compromise integrity for revenue.
I reinvest in my business strategically rather than extracting all profits.
I view my business resources as entrusted to me, not just owned by me.

dims.name: Team & Culture Building
questions: I invest in hiring the right people and developing them.
My team members feel valued, not just used.
I have created a workplace culture aligned with my values.
I lead my team with clear expectations, fair compensation, and genuine care.

dims.name: Social Impact
questions: My business positively impacts the community beyond just employment.
I intentionally create opportunities for underserved or marginalised people.
I consider the environmental and social effects of my business decisions.
I use my platform and profits to contribute to causes I believe in.

dims.name: Succession & Exit Strategy
questions: I am building a business that could function without me.
I have thought about my long-term exit — sale, succession, or transition.
I document my knowledge and processes so others can carry them forward.
I am developing leaders within my business who could take over key roles.

pillars.name: Pillar 1: Self-Discovery (Who Am I?)
pillars.subtitle: The starting point for all growth

dims.name: Identity & Self-Worth
questions: I have a healthy, grounded sense of who I am — independent of titles or opinions.
I do not define my worth by my achievements, appearance, or what others think of me.
I can name what makes me unique and valuable without arrogance.
I am comfortable in my own skin and don't feel the need to perform for acceptance.

dims.name: Personality & Strengths
questions: I know my personality type and understand how it affects my behaviour.
I can name my top strengths and I actively use them in my daily life.
I understand my weaknesses and manage them rather than pretending they don't exist.
I have taken at least one formal assessment (personality, strengths, or gifts).

dims.name: Values & Beliefs
questions: I can clearly articulate my top 5 core values.
My daily decisions and actions are aligned with my stated values.
I have examined my beliefs critically rather than simply inheriting them.
When my actions conflict with my values, I notice and correct course.

dims.name: Purpose & Calling
questions: I have a sense of purpose — I know why I'm here.
I can connect my daily activities to a bigger picture or mission.
I feel energised and alive when I am operating in my purpose.
I am actively pursuing my calling, not just waiting for it to find me.
I can distinguish between what I'm good at and what I'm called to.

pillars.name: Pillar 2: Inner Healing (Becoming Whole)
pillars.subtitle: You can't build a new life on unhealed foundations

dims.name: Identifying Past Wounds
questions: I am aware of specific past experiences that still affect me emotionally today.
I can identify patterns in my life (relationships, reactions, fears) linked to past pain.
I have reflected on how my childhood and upbringing shaped my current behaviours.
I recognise when I am reacting from a wounded place rather than a whole place.

dims.name: Forgiveness & Release
questions: I have forgiven those who have hurt me, even if I haven't forgotten.
I do not carry active bitterness or resentment toward specific people.
I have released the need for revenge, vindication, or an apology I may never receive.
I practise forgiveness as a choice, not a feeling.

dims.name: Overcoming Shame & Rejection
questions: I do not carry a deep sense of shame about who I am.
Rejection from others does not devastate me or define my self-image.
I can distinguish between guilt (I did something wrong) and shame (I am something wrong).
I have experienced healing or freedom from specific shame-based narratives.

dims.name: Breaking Unhealthy Patterns
questions: I have identified recurring negative patterns in my life (addictions, toxic relationships, self-sabotage).
I am actively working to break at least one destructive cycle.
I understand the root causes behind my unhealthy coping mechanisms.
I have replaced at least one unhealthy habit with a healthy alternative.

dims.name: Restoration & Wholeness
questions: I feel like I am moving from surviving to thriving.
I experience inner peace and emotional stability most of the time.
I can talk about my past without being overwhelmed by it.
I am becoming the person I was created to be — healed, whole, and free.

pillars.name: Pillar 3: Whole-Person Health (Spirit, Soul & Body)
pillars.subtitle: An integrated system — one affects all

dims.name: Spiritual Life
questions: I have a consistent spiritual practice (prayer, meditation, worship) that nourishes me.
I experience genuine peace and connection in my spiritual life.
My faith positively influences my decisions and relationships.
I am growing spiritually — I am not stagnant or going through the motions.

dims.name: Emotional Health
questions: I am aware of my emotions and can name what I'm feeling in the moment.
I process difficult emotions rather than suppressing or exploding with them.
I have healthy outlets for stress, sadness, and frustration.
I can experience joy, gratitude, and contentment regularly.

dims.name: Mental Wellness
questions: My thought patterns are generally healthy and constructive.
I manage anxiety and worry effectively rather than being controlled by them.
I can focus and think clearly without chronic mental fog or overwhelm.
I would seek professional help if my mental health needed it, without stigma.

dims.name: Physical Health
questions: I exercise or move my body regularly as part of my lifestyle.
I eat in a way that nourishes and energises me.
I get enough quality sleep most nights.
I take care of my physical health proactively — checkups, rest, hydration.

pillars.name: Pillar 4: Relational Intelligence (How Do I Connect?)
pillars.subtitle: No one grows alone

dims.name: Communication Skills
questions: I express myself clearly and honestly without being hurtful.
I am a good listener — I hear people out before responding.
I adapt my communication style depending on who I'm speaking with.
I can have difficult conversations without avoiding or attacking.

dims.name: Conflict Management
questions: I address conflict directly rather than avoiding or suppressing it.
I can disagree with someone without damaging the relationship.
I seek understanding before trying to be understood in disagreements.
I don't hold grudges — I resolve issues and move forward.

dims.name: Boundaries
questions: I can say 'no' without guilt when something doesn't align with my priorities.
I know the difference between being kind and being a pushover.
I have healthy boundaries with family, friends, and work.
I respect other people's boundaries as much as I expect them to respect mine.

dims.name: Community & Belonging
questions: I have meaningful relationships where I am known and loved.
I invest intentionally in building and maintaining friendships.
I belong to a community (church, group, network) that supports my growth.
I don't isolate myself — I allow others to speak into my life.

pillars.name: Pillar 5: Purpose & Productivity (Am I Moving Forward?)
pillars.subtitle: From self-improvement to contribution

dims.name: Goal Setting & Planning
questions: I set clear, specific goals for different areas of my life.
I break big goals into small, actionable steps with timelines.
I review my goals regularly and track my progress.
I adjust my goals when circumstances change rather than abandoning them.

dims.name: Time Management & Discipline
questions: I manage my time intentionally rather than just reacting to what comes up.
I prioritise what's important over what's urgent.
I have daily routines and habits that support my goals.
I avoid chronic procrastination and time-wasting activities.

dims.name: Financial Literacy
questions: I have a personal budget and I generally stick to it.
I understand the basics of saving, investing, and managing debt.
I make financial decisions based on planning, not impulse.
I am building financial stability rather than living paycheck to paycheck.

dims.name: Career & Vocation Clarity
questions: I am clear on what kind of work energises me and fits my strengths.
I am actively developing skills that will advance my career or calling.
I am in the right lane professionally — or I have a plan to get there.
I don't just work for money — I work for meaning and contribution.

dims.name: Leadership Readiness & Legacy
questions: I am ready and willing to lead others, not just manage myself.
I think about the impact I want to have on the world around me.
I serve and give back to my community or church regularly.
I live with the end in mind — my daily choices reflect my long-term vision.

dims.name: Learning & Skill Development
questions: I am actively learning new skills relevant to my goals and calling.
I read, listen to podcasts, or take courses regularly to grow.
I invest time and money in my own development.
I apply what I learn rather than just consuming information passively.
